displayTitle: The Number You Carry, and What It Owes You

beats.narration: Your pocket dosimeter is a running score of dose. Reading it, logging it, and acting on it is the job behind the badge.

payload.title: KEEPING SCORE
payload.sub: Read it. Log it. Act on it.

beats.narration: Charge it to exactly zero each morning, then point the eyepiece at any light and read the fiber's place on the scale, straight on, often.

tiles.s: After each shot, before you leave

beats.narration: Log the difference between start and end as the day's dose. But if the fiber sits past full scale, your dose is unknown.

payload.heading: Off scale means dose unknown
payload.note: PAST FULL SCALE = UNKNOWN, NOT 200 mR

beats.narration: When it reads off scale, the response is fixed: stop work, notify the safety officer, process your badge, stay out until dose is known.

chips.s: Secure the source, take no shots
Until your real dose is known

beats.narration: Limits are ceilings, never budgets. Five rem a year is where things already went wrong. ALARA — as low as reasonably achievable — sets your real pace.

payload.sub: As low as reasonably achievable — not as much as allowed.
